AI agentic shell for Bash & PowerShell

g.text: 🤖 Ready to restore on new machine.

svg.text: 5 new utilities reviewed • Complete migration workflow tested • WSL backup strategies compared
The personal computer that runs both CP/M and MS-DOS.

text.tspan: Run your existing CP/M-80 software on the built-in Z80 processor —
then switch to MS-DOS for the latest business applications.
256KB RAM standard, expandable to 896KB. Dual 400KB floppies.
Optional 10MB Winchester. Full networking with DECnet.

svg.text: Circle number 1 on the reader service card.

g.text: PCmigrate's dark-themed WPF interface shows live export progress.
The split Export button offers WSL-only and bundle modes.

text.tspan: M
oving to a new Windows PC has always been an exercise in tedium. You spend the first weekend reinstalling
applications, hunting for license keys scrawled on Post-it notes, and praying your WSL environment survives the
journey. PCmigrate, a free open-source utility from developer PFWorks, compresses that weekend into fifteen minutes.
SETUP AND INTERFACE
You can grab PCmigrate as a traditional installer or portable zip from
GitHub Releases. The installer weighs under 2 MB. On first launch it
requests administrator privileges and presents a dark-themed WPF
window that feels surprisingly polished for a PowerShell application.
The interface is minimal: a path selector, an Export button with a
dropdown for mode selection, and a Restore button. No wizard, no
account creation, no telemetry. We appreciated the restraint.
EXPORT PROCESS
We tested on a Dell Precision workstation running Windows 11 23H2
with 47 Win32 apps, 12 Store apps, two WSL distributions (Ubuntu
22.04 on WSL 2 and Alpine on WSL 1), and license keys in the registry.
Clicking Export Only with the output aimed at a Samsung T7 SSD took
4 minutes 38 seconds. The tool produced a CSV inventory, interactive
HTML checklist with download links, winget JSON export, full Ubuntu
VHDX and Alpine tar, .wslconfig, zipped AppData folders, and a
generated restore script.
The HTML checklist deserves special mention. It renders a dark-themed
page with checkboxes. Check off an app and the row grays out. License
keys hide behind a click-to-reveal toggle with a per-key copy button.
Small touch, real time saved.

g.text: The industry-standard compression utility. Shrink files up to
New Deflate algorithm. DOS, OS/2, Windows. Only $47.
Circle number 72 on the reader service card.

text.tspan: WSL BACKUP
The WSL-Only mode skips software inventory and just exports your
Linux distributions. For developers who routinely break their WSL
setup experimenting with kernel builds, this is a one-click safety net.
We exported a 14 GB Ubuntu VHDX to USB 3.2 in just over two
minutes—limited entirely by sequential write speed. On restore the
tool creates install directories, runs wsl --import with correct flags,
and prints a reminder to set your default user.
RESTORE
We moved the Samsung T7 to a fresh Windows 11 machine. Winget
imported 39 of 47 packages (the remainder had changed IDs or were
unavailable). Both WSL distributions came back intact, including shell
history and SSH keys. The entire restore took 11 minutes on gigabit.
ROUGH EDGES
SmartScreen flags the unsigned installer, which will alarm non-
technical users. No scheduled backup option yet—you must remember
to run exports manually. A Task Scheduler integration would round
out the WSL backup story nicely.
CONCLUSIONS
PCmigrate fills the gap Microsoft left when it retired Windows Easy
Transfer. It's free, fast, respects your privacy, and handles WSL—
the one thing most migration tools ignore entirely. Whether you're
moving to a new machine or want insurance before your next rm -rf
mishap, PCmigrate earns our Editors' Choice.
Product:
PCmigrate 0.4
Company:
PFWorks
Price:
Free (MIT License)
Requires:
Windows 10 v1709+ or 11, PowerShell 5.1+,
Administrator privileges, winget
Download:
github.com/pfworks/PCmigrate/releases

g.text: Circle number 203 on the reader service card.

text.tspan: Full 16-bit ISA bus mastering. 10Base-T and BNC connectors.
Hardware-selectable IRQ (3, 5, 7, 9, 10, 11). 16KB on-board
RAM buffer. NetWare, LAN Manager, and TCP/IP drivers included.

g.text: Suggested retail. See your dealer or call 1-800-SMC-4-YOU.
Circle number 118 on the reader service card.

text.tspan: W
hat if your shell could write its own commands?
sheLLaMa integrates LLMs directly into bash and
PowerShell. Type a natural-language request prefixed
with a comma, and the AI proposes commands, executes
them (with approval), reads output, and iterates up to
10 rounds. Supports local LLMs via Ollama, cloud fall-
back, and distributed backends. Runs fully offline after
model pull. Permission tiers block destructive commands
while read-only ops auto-execute.
Beyond the shell: web GUI, REST API, OpenAI-compat-
ible endpoint (works with Cursor), image generation,
cloud cost estimation. Ambitious but cohesive.
We tested with Ollama + Llama 3 on RTX 4090.
Response times: 1.2s simple, 4-6s for agentic loops.
Conversation save/load, branching, and auto-compac-
tion are genuinely useful. The PowerShell integration is
equally capable — rare for AI tools that treat Windows
as an afterthought. File editing uses diff-based search-
and-replace with previews. Web search lets the AI look
up docs mid-task. Admin console shows token stats,
costs, backend health. Half star off for setup complexity.

svg.text: Circle number 205 on the reader service card.

text.tspan: Red Hat dropped lsb_release from RHEL 9, breaking
countless scripts. This drop-in shell replacement parses
/etc/os-release and provides identical output. Install it,
your automation works again. No dependencies beyond
bash. Dead simple, exactly as it should be.

svg.text: Circle number 206 on the reader service card.

text.tspan: Testing DHCP on bridged or VLAN-tagged interfaces is
painful. This script takes a MAC and bridge name, fires
a DHCP request, shows what happened. TUI mode
(--tui) gives split-screen real-time packet capture.
Invaluable for libvirt/KVM network debugging.

svg.text: Circle number 207 on the reader service card.

INTERNAL TAPE BACKUP — NEVER LOSE YOUR DATA AGAIN.
60MB QIC-36 streaming tape. Backs up a full 40MB hard disk in 8 minutes. ISA card + drive. Software included. $495.
Everex Systems • Fremont, CA • Circle number 131 on the reader service card.

text.tspan: After reading your preview of PCmigrate, I set it up for WSL-only backups. Last week I ran 'rm -rf /' inside my
Ubuntu instance instead of the container I was targeting. PCmigrate had a 3-hour-old VHDX on my USB. Restored
in two minutes. — D. Markham, Portland, OR
We've run polyDBackup for three months. The verification step flagged a checksum mismatch — turned out a bad
RAM stick was flipping bits during dumps. Without checksums, we'd have restored corrupted backups and never
known. The staging safety model gives our DBA peace of mind. — R. Vasquez, Miami, FL
I was using Copilot CLI, a separate Ollama wrapper, and ChatGPT in a browser. sheLLaMa replaced all three. The
agentic loop is genuinely useful for sysadmin tasks, and it runs fully local. No data leaves my network.
— K. Johansson, Stockholm, Sweden

svg.text: 8-BIT ISA ETHERNET FOR EVERY PC IN YOUR OFFICE.

text.tspan: Works in any IBM PC, XT, AT, or compatible with a single 8-bit slot.
10Base-2 (thin coax) and AUI connectors. On-board 8KB buffer.
Hardware-configurable IRQ and I/O base address. Novell NetWare certified.
Includes drivers for NetWare 2.x/3.x, NDIS, and Packet Driver spec.
Full 802.3 compliance. 5-year limited warranty.

On your desk. Not in it.

text.tspan: The MiniScribe 8450XT gives you 40 megabytes of fast, reliable storage without
opening your PC. Simply connect the external enclosure to the included half-length
ISA controller card, plug in the power supply, and you're running in minutes.
■ 40MB formatted capacity (53MB unformatted)
■ 28ms average access time
■ 5¼" half-height form factor
■ RLL or MFM encoding (factory configured)
■ Whisper-quiet operation (40 dBA)
■ External power supply included
■ Half-length 8-bit ISA controller card
■ Works with PC, XT, AT, and compatibles
■ DOS 3.x and 4.x compatible
■ 1-year limited warranty

svg.text: Drive + enclosure + controller + cables + software. Ready to run.
"The easiest hard disk installation we've ever seen. Plug in and go."
Compatible with IBM PC, PC/XT, PC/AT, PS/2 Model 30, and 100% compatibles including Compaq, Tandy, Epson, Leading Edge,
Zenith, and AT&T. Not compatible with Micro Channel (PS/2 Model 50 and above).
Available at ComputerLand, Businessland, MicroAge, and leading computer dealers nationwide.
Circle number 47 on the reader service card.
